<USER_REQUEST>
ROLE

You are a senior full-stack engineer. Build a production-quality AI Investment Research Agent as a single deployable Next.js application. Follow this spec exactly unless something is genuinely ambiguous — if so, make the most sensible production-grade choice and note it in the README under "Key decisions & trade-offs."

OBJECTIVE

A user types a company name into a web UI. The app runs a LangGraph.js agent that researches the company (recent news/sentiment, financial fundamentals, competitive landscape, risk factors), then produces a single INVEST or PASS decision with a confidence score, a plain-English rationale, key supporting/opposing points, and cited sources. The user watches the agent's research steps happen live, not just a spinner.

MANDATORY TECH STACK


Next.js 14+ App Router, TypeScript, React 18 — single app, frontend + backend (API routes) together
LangGraph.js (@langchain/langgraph) for agent orchestration — this is the core of the assignment, use it properly (typed state, real graph topology, not a disguised linear function chain)
LangChain.js core (@langchain/core) + one model provider package (@langchain/openai or @langchain/anthropic) — make the provider swappable via env var, don't hardcode one
Zod for structured LLM output and API validation
Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui for styling
Tavily Search API (@langchain/community/tools/tavily_search or the tavily npm SDK) for live web/news research — free tier exists, good LangChain.js integration
A financial fundamentals API — Financial Modeling Prep or Alpha Vantage free tier (company overview, key metrics, income statement basics)
Deploy target: Vercel


ENV VARS (produce a .env.example)

# LLM provider - pick one, code should branch on LLM_PROVIDER
LLM_PROVIDER=openai            # "openai" | "anthropic"
OPENAI_API_KEY=
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=
MODEL_NAME=gpt-4o-mini          # set to current model slug for whichever provider you pick — check provider docs, model names change often

# Research tools
TAVILY_API_KEY=
FMP_API_
<truncated 9040 bytes>
ploy steps if deployed
How it works — explain the LangGraph topology in prose (the fan-out/fan-in shape above), what each node does, why structured output is used for the decision, how partial-data / private-company cases are handled
Key decisions & trade-offs — e.g. why Tavily over raw scraping, why fan-out research nodes instead of one giant prompt, why a free-tier fundamentals API was chosen and its limitations, what was explicitly left out and why (e.g. no historical price charts, no multi-turn chat/follow-up questions, no persistence layer) given the 7-day scope
Example runs — after building, actually run the agent against 3 real companies of your choice (mix a large public one, a smaller/regional public one, and one that will hit the insufficient-data path) and paste the real decision + confidence + reasoning output for each
What you would improve with more time — e.g. persistence/history of past reports, multi-model comparison, portfolio-level analysis across several companies, backtesting the agent's calls, human-in-the-loop feedback before finalizing a decision
Note at the bottom: AI build chat transcripts are included in docs/chat-transcripts/ (this is a submission requirement — remind me to actually save them there, don't fabricate transcript content)


FINAL DELIVERABLE

A working Next.js app that runs with npm install && npm run dev, uses LangGraph.js with real branching topology (not a linear disguise), streams live progress to the UI, produces a defensible INVEST/PASS decision grounded only in data it actually gathered, and ships with the README above fully filled in with real example runs.


</USER_REQUEST>

"# AI Investment Research Agent Implementation Plan

This plan details the implementation of a production-quality AI Investment Research Agent as a deployable Next.js application, meeting all constraints and structural requirements.

## Goal
Build a Next.js application that takes a company name, runs a LangGraph.js agent to perform concurrent research (news, financials, competitors, risks), synthesizes the findings, and makes a final INVEST/PASS decision with reasoning. The agent streams its progress live to the frontend using Server-Sent Events (SSE).

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The app requires API keys for OpenAI/Anthropic, Tavily, and Financial Modeling Prep. I will configure `.env.example` as required, but you will need to supply the real `.env` values when running locally.

> [!NOTE]
> The default project will be created in `investment-research-agent/` in the workspace root. We will use Next.js 14 App Router, shadcn/ui, and `npm` as the package manager.

## Proposed Changes

### Setup and Scaffolding
- Initialize the Next.js app using `npx create-next-app@latest investment-research-agent --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir false --import-alias "@/*" --use-npm`
- Initialize `shadcn/ui` with `npx shadcn@latest init` and add required components (tabs, card, badge, progress, etc.).
- Install required dependencies: `@langchain/langgraph`, `@langchain/core`, `@langchain/openai`, `@langchain/anthropic`, `zod`, `@langchain/community`, `tavily`, etc.

### Core Architecture (Backend/Agent)
The core logic will reside in `lib/agent/`:
- **State Definition (`state.ts`)**: Define the `AgentState` using `Annotation.Root` with the exact schema requested, including custom reducers for parallel branch fan-in and error appending.
- **Graph Topology (`graph.ts`)**: Construct a `StateGraph` with the precise nodes:
  - `START -> resolveCompany`
  - Conditional branch on `resolveCompany` output:
    - Resolvable: Fan-out to `gatherNews`, `gatherFinancials`, `gatherCompetit

You are an expert investment research assistant.

Identify if this is a real, researchable company/entity.

Return your response as a JSON object matching this structure:
{{
  "resolved": boolean,
  "name": string,
  "ticker": string | null,
  "isPublic": boolean
}}
Do not include any formatting or other text, return ONLY the raw JSON string.
`;

export const NEWS_RESEARCH_PROMPT = `
You are an expert financial analyst focusing on recent news and sentiment.
Analyze the following news search results for "{companyName}":

{searchResults}

Summarize:
1. The key themes in recent news (last 3-6 months).
2. The overall sentiment (positive, neutral, or negative).
3. Any significant events (earnings, product launches, management changes, controversies).

Provide a concise summary, the overall sentiment, and the list of sources used (URL links).
`;

3. What is the company's market position?

Provide a concise summary, a list of primary competitors, and the sources used.
`;

## Overview
An autonomous, production-grade AI Investment Research Agent built as a single deployable Next.js 14+ App Router application. The app leverages LangGraph.js to orchestrate concurrent research nodes analyzing news, sentiment, financials, competitors, and risk factors. It uses a structured LLM output model to formulate a final investment decision (INVEST or PASS) with a confidence score, detailed reasoning, and cited source links. The UI provides a live, step-by-step checklist showing the agent's progress in real-time.

## How to Run It

### 1. Clone & Install
```bash
git clone <repository-url>
cd investment-research-agent
npm install --legacy-peer-deps
```

### 2. Configure Environment Variables
Create a `.env` file in the root of the project and populate it with your keys:
```ini
LLM_PROVIDER=openai            # "openai" | "anthropic"
OPENAI_API_KEY=your-openai-api-key
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-anthropic-api-key # Required if LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic
MODEL_NAME=gpt-4o-mini          # gpt-4o-mini or claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022

TAVILY_API_KEY=your-tavily-api-key
FMP_API_KEY=your-fmp-api-key    # Financial Modeling Prep API Key (free tier works)
```

### 3. Run Locally
```bash
npm run dev
```
Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) to view and test the application.

### 4. Vercel Deployment
Deploy directly to Vercel:
1. Push the code to a GitHub repository.
2. Link the repository on the Vercel Dashboard.
3. Configure all environment variables in Vercel Project Settings.
4. Deploy the project.

---

## How It Works

### LangGraph.js Topology
The core engine utilizes a real branching StateGraph orchestrated by `@langchain/langgraph`:
```
                     [START]
                        │
                  resolveCompany
                 /              \
         (resolved)         (unresolved)
         /    │    \   \          \
  gatherNews  │   gatherRisks   insufficientData
  gatherFinancials

I have completed building the **AI Investment Research Agent** as a single deployable Next.js 14+ application inside the `investment-research-agent/` folder.

Here is a summary of the implementation:
1. **Scaffolded Next.js 14+ App Router & UI**: Configured with Tailwind CSS, Lucide React icons, and a custom set of shadcn/ui components (`Badge`, `Card`, `Tabs`, `Progress`) styled with a premium dark glassmorphic palette.
2. **LangGraph.js Orchestration**: Created a typed `StateGraph` which resolves company identity, then routes to 4 concurrent research tasks (News, Financials, Competitors, and Risks) before fanning back in to synthesize the brief, evaluate a verdict via structured output schemas (`decide`), and compile a professional markdown report.
3. **SSE Streaming Router**: Constructed a Server-Sent Events route (`/api/research`) that runs the LangGraph stream, emitting progress updates, errors/warnings, and final result records.
4. **Resiliency & Fallbacks**: Integrated robust error boundary checking. If FMP API keys are missing or a private company is supplied, the agent falls back to web searches or reports private company status without crashing. An `insufficientData` node handles fictional queries safely.
5. **Types and Build Verification**: Re-configured typing schemas to pass TypeScript compilation checks. The Next.js production build (`npm run build`) compiles successfully without any warnings.
6. **Deliverables**: Added a detailed `README.md` file in the project folder and copied the session's chat transcript to `docs/chat-transcripts/transcript.jsonl`.
